macro_segments.title: Three Positions on Stolen Pregnant Animal or Sheared Sheep: Rabbi Meir, Rabbi Yehuda, and Rabbi Shimon

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir's Position: Return with Offspring and Shearings as Penalty
Rabbi Yehuda's Position: Return in Current State, Owner Gets Increase
Rabbi Shimon's Position: Assess Value, Assess Applies to Increase

macro_segments.title: Dispute Between Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Shimon on Ownership of Improvement (Shevach) to Stolen Object

micro_segments.title: Zevid's Interpretation: Dispute Over Who Owns Improvement When Not Yet Sheared
Rav Pappa's Version: All Agree Gazlan Owns Improvement, Dispute Over Portion

macro_segments.title: Tosafot's Explanation of Shevach Retention as Takkanat Hashavim (Enactment for Repentant Thieves)

micro_segments.title: Tosafot: Improvement Retention Not Based on Shinuy Koneh but on Enactment
Purpose: Incentivize Gazlan to Return Principal by Allowing Him Benefit

macro_segments.title: Dispute Between Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbi Shimon on Ownership of Improvement (Shevach) to Stolen Object (continued)

micro_segments.title: Clarification: Half, Third, or Quarter Portion Depends on Local Custom

macro_segments.title: Three Cases Where Improvement Can Be Separated by Payment: Bechor, Creditor, Orphans

micro_segments.title: Bechor to Pashut: Eldest Son's Share Increased, Siblings Not Remaining Partners
Creditor Collecting from Purchaser: Assessment of Increase Without Partnership
Creditor of Orphans: Similar Principle, Purchaser Paid Off Rather Than Remaining Partner

macro_segments.title: Contradiction in Shmuel's Rulings on Creditor's Right to Collected Improvement

micro_segments.title: First Statement: Creditor Must Reimburse Purchaser for Improvements Made
Second Statement: Creditor Collects Improvement Without Reimbursement
Resolution: Distinction Between Improvement Ready for Harvest (Magiah L'Ktefayim) and Natural Growth

macro_segments.title: Tosafot's Alternative Explanation: Improvement Through Labor vs. Natural Growth

micro_segments.title: Tosafot: Magiah L'Ktefayim Means Improvement Requiring Labor, Not Ready Harvest

macro_segments.title: Resolution Based on Loan Amount: When Creditor Must Pay Purchaser vs. Can Seize All

micro_segments.title: If Loan Smaller Than Land Value: Creditor Pays Purchaser for Excess
If Loan Equals or Exceeds Land Value: Creditor Seizes Entire Property

macro_segments.title: Debate on Purchaser's Right to Reject Payment and Retain His Share of Land

micro_segments.title: One Position: Purchaser with Money Can Force Creditor to Accept Payment
Opposite Position: Purchaser Cannot Force Creditor to Accept Payment
Special Case of Apotiki: Designated Collateral Strengthens Creditor's Rights

macro_segments.title: Rights of Gazlan and Subsequent Purchaser to Stolen Object's Improvement

micro_segments.title: Gazlan Transfers All Rights to Purchaser: First Seller Passes All Acquired Rights
Non-Jewish Purchaser: Question of Whether Enactment Applied to Non-Jews
Complex Chain: Jewish Gazlan to Non-Jew to Jewish Purchaser and Continuity of Rights

macro_segments.title: Shinuy Koneh (Acquisition Through Modification): Palm Tree to Wood and Progressive Transformations

micro_segments.title: Palm Tree Chopped Down: Still Called Palm Tree, Not Sufficient Shinuy
Palm Logs: Not Yet Shinuy Koneh, Still Called Logs of Palm
Logs to Beams: Sufficient Shinuy Koneh, Thief Acquires
Beam to Smaller Beam: Insufficient Shinuy, Same Category of Object
Beams to Planks: Sufficient Shinuy Koneh, Thief Acquires

macro_segments.title: Shinuy Koneh with Lulab: Transformations and Reversibility Principle

micro_segments.title: Lulab to Broom (Hutzi): Changed Name, Sufficient Shinuy Koneh
Broom Split Leaves (Chufya): More Labor Intensive, Sufficient Shinuy Koneh
Chufya to Rope (Sharshura): Reversible Modification, Not Shinuy Koneh

macro_segments.title: Principle of Shinuy Hachozer Livriyato: Reversible Changes Do Not Create Acquisition

micro_segments.title: Dust to Brick: Reversible (Can Crush Back to Dust), Not Shinuy Koneh
Brick to Dust: Irreversible (New Brick Different), Shinuy Koneh
Metal to Coin: Reversible, Not Shinuy Koneh
Coin to Metal: Irreversible (New Coin Different), Shinuy Koneh
Blackened Coin to Polished: Reversible (Can Blacken Again), Not Shinuy Koneh
Polished Coin to Blackened: Not Fully Reversible (Loss of Silver), Shinuy Koneh

macro_segments.title: Principle Summary: All Gazlanim Pay Based on Value at Time of Theft (Keshat Hag'zeilah)

micro_segments.title: Universal Rule: Gazlanim Reimburse Original Value When Thief Fails to Create Shinuy
Exception: Sheep or Calf Developing Naturally During Theft Creates Shinuy Koneh

macro_segments.title: Case: Gazlan Uses Stolen Oxen to Plow and Increase Land Value; Rav Nachman's Ruling

micro_segments.title: Scenario: Thief Returns Oxen, Claims No Payment Owed Since Oxen Returned
Rav Nachman's Ruling: Gazlan Must Reimburse Owner for Land Improvement Caused by Stolen Oxen

macro_segments.title: Rava's Challenge to Rav Nachman: Oxen Contributed to but Did Not Solely Cause Improvement

micro_segments.title: Rava's First Objection: Oxen Only Partially Responsible for Land Improvement
Rav Nachman's Clarification: Pay Half the Land Improvement Value
Rava's Second Objection: Returns in Original Form, So Should Only Pay Original Value

macro_segments.title: Rav Nachman's Response: Habitual Gazlan Requires Extra Punishment Beyond Standard Rules

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman Claims Judicial Discretion: Do Not Second-Guess When Sitting in Judgment
Reasoning: Old Habitual Gazlan Requires Fine Beyond Standard Rules as Deterrent

topical_tags.term: Keshat Hag'zeilah (Value at Time of Theft)
Rishi/Rash (First and Second Parties in Mishna)
Panim Chadashos Ba'u L'Chan (New Form Created)
